title: ANT/ENT Endings and -mente Adverbs
description: Convert -ant/-ent endings to Spanish by adding -e. Learn to form adverbs with -mente.

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: What was 'is' or 'he is', 'she is', 'it is'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Es. Good. Words ending in -ant or -ent often become Spanish by adding -e. For example, 'important' becomes importante. How would you say 'it is important'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Es importante. How would you say 'it's not important'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: No es importante. How would you say 'different'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Diferente. It's not different:

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: No es diferente. How would you say 'constant'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Constante. How would you say 'it is constant'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Es constante. Add -mente to form adverbs. 'Constantly' is constantemente. How would you say 'legally'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Legalmente. Good. 'Illegally'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Ilegalmente. How do you say 'real' in Spanish?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Real. How would you say 'really'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Realmente. 'Normally' is normalmente. How would you say 'normally it's important'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Normalmente es importante. If a word has a /j/ sound, it becomes /kh/ in Spanish. How would you say 'global'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Global. 'Globally'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Globalmente. 'General' becomes?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: General. 'Generally'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Generalmente. 'Original'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Original. 'Originally'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Originalmente. 'Digital'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Digital. 'Naturally'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Naturalmente. How would you say 'naturally it's original'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Naturalmente es original. Words ending in -ible or -able often convert by pronouncing syllable by syllable with stress on the second last syllable. 'Possible' becomes posible. How would you say 'probably'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Probablemente. 'Possibly'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Posiblemente. 'Flexible'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Flexible. Remember, experiment with vocabulary conversion, but be aware of exceptions. This approach can help you quickly expand your Spanish vocabulary.

outro.text: You've learned to convert English words ending in -ant, -ent, -ible, and -able into Spanish.
Try converting more words like 'elegant' or 'responsible' into Spanish.
You've also learned to form adverbs with -mente, allowing you to express actions more precisely.
Keep experimenting with these patterns to build your vocabulary and confidence in Spanish.
